input.content:
# GIFTI-RS Feature Parity Documentation

## Overview
This document tracks the feature parity between the R `gifti` package and the Rust `gifti-rs` implementation. The goal is to achieve 100% functional compatibility while leveraging Rust's performance and safety advantages.

## R Package Analysis

### Core Functionality (To be analyzed)
- [ ] GIFTI file reading/parsing
- [ ] GIFTI file writing/serialization
- [ ] XML structure validation
- [ ] Data array handling
- [ ] Metadata processing
- [ ] Error handling and validation

### R Functions to Port (Initial Assessment)
*Note: This section will be populated after examining the R package structure*

| R Function | Purpose | Complexity | Rust Equivalent | Status |
|------------|---------|------------|----------------|---------|
| TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | Pending |

### Data Structures to Port

| R Structure | Purpose | Rust Equivalent | Status |
|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
| TBD | TBD | TBD | Pending |

## Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1: Core Data Structures
- [ ] Define GIFTI file structure types
- [ ] Implement XML parsing/serialization
- [ ] Create error handling framework
- [ ] Set up basic I/O operations

### Phase 2: Functional Compatibility
- [ ] Port reading functions
- [ ] Port writing functions
- [ ] Implement validation logic
- [ ] Add metadata handling

### Phase 3: Performance & Safety
- [ ] Optimize memory usage
- [ ] Add parallel processing where applicable
- [ ] Implement comprehensive error handling
- [ ] Add property-based testing

## Testing Strategy

### Test Coverage Goals
- [ ] Unit tests for all core functions
- [ ] Integration tests with R-generated fixtures
- [ ] Property-based tests for edge cases
- [ ] Performance benchmarks vs R implementation

### Cross-Language Validation
- [ ] Set up R test fixture generation
- [ ] Implement equivalence testing framework
- [ ] Add numerical tolerance handling
- [ ] Create performance comparison suite

## Current Status

### Completed Features
*None yet - project initialization phase*

### In Progress
- Project structure setup
- Documentation creation
- R package analysis planning

### Pending
- All core functionality
- Testing framework
- Performance optimization
- Documentation completion

## Success Metrics

### Functional Parity
- [ ] 100% R function coverage
- [ ] Identical output for all test cases
- [ ] Complete error handling compatibility
- [ ] Full metadata preservation

### Performance Goals
- [ ] ≤20% performance difference from R (or faster)
- [ ] Memory usage optimization
- [ ] Parallel processing where beneficial
- [ ] Zero-cost abstractions

### Quality Assurance
- [ ] >95% test coverage
- [ ] Zero unsafe code (or fully documented)
- [ ] Comprehensive rustdoc documentation
- [ ] Passes all clippy lints

## Dependencies

### Core Dependencies (Planned)
- `serde` + `serde_xml_rs` - XML serialization
- `thiserror` - Error handling
- `ndarray` - Multi-dimensional arrays
- `byteorder` - Binary data handling

### Development Dependencies (Planned)
- `criterion` - Benchmarking
- `proptest` - Property-based testing
- `tempfile` - Test utilities
- `approx` - Numerical comparisons

## Notes
- This document will be updated as the R package analysis progresses
- Feature complexity will be assessed during implementation
- Performance benchmarks will be added after core functionality is complete

# GIFTI-RS Development Progress

## Project Overview
Rust implementation of the R `gifti` package for reading and writing GIFTI (Geometry Format under the Neuroimaging Informatics Technology Initiative) files.

## Development Timeline

### 2025-07-16 - Project Initialization
- ✅ Created project structure in `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/rust/gifti-rs/`
- ✅ Read and understood R2Rust unified conversion guide
- ✅ Created `feature_parity.md` for tracking R package compatibility
- ✅ Created `progress.md` for development tracking
- ⏳ Planning to clone reference R repository: https://github.com/muschellij2/gifti

### Current Sprint Goals
1. **Discovery Phase** (In Progress)
   - [ ] Clone and examine R `gifti` package structure
   - [ ] Analyze GIFTI file format specifications
   - [ ] Inventory R package functions and data structures
   - [ ] Assess implementation complexity

2. **Architecture Phase** (Planned)
   - [ ] Design Rust library structure
   - [ ] Define core data types
   - [ ] Plan error handling strategy
   - [ ] Select appropriate dependencies

   - [ ] Implement GIFTI file reading
   - [ ] Implement GIFTI file writing
   - [ ] Add validation and error handling
   - [ ] Create test suite

## Development Log

### Today's Work
- **09:00-09:30**: Project setup and documentation creation
- **Status**: Setting up development environment and planning approach

### Key Decisions Made
- Following R2Rust unified conversion guide for best practices
- Prioritizing feature parity documentation before implementation
- Using progress tracking to maintain development visibility

### Blockers/Issues
- None currently identified

### Next Steps
1. Clone reference R repository for analysis
2. Examine GIFTI file format and R package structure
3. Begin architectural design based on findings

*To be populated as development progresses*

- **Performance Benchmarks**: 0 written

- ✅ `feature_parity.md` - Created (basic structure)
- ✅ `progress.md` - Created (this document)
- ⏳ `README.md` - Planned
- ⏳ `API docs` - Planned (rustdoc)

## Performance Targets

- **Processing Speed**: Target ≤20% slower than R (or faster)
- **File I/O**: TBD
- **Parallel Processing**: TBD

- Maintaining comprehensive documentation throughout development

file.content:
Package: gifti
Type: Package
Title: Reads in 'Neuroimaging' 'GIFTI' Files with Geometry Information
Version: 0.8.2
Date: 2021-03-31
Author: John Muschelli
Maintainer: John Muschelli <muschellij2@gmail.com>
Description: Functions to read in the geometry format under the 
    'Neuroimaging' 'Informatics' Technology Initiative ('NIfTI'), called 
    'GIFTI' <https://www.nitrc.org/projects/gifti/>. 
    These files contain surfaces of brain imaging data.
License: GPL-2
Imports:
    xml2 (>= 1.1.1),
    base64enc,
    R.utils,
    tools,
    utils
Suggests:
    rgl,
    grDevices,
    testthat,
    knitr,
    rmarkdown,
    covr
BugReports: https://github.com/muschellij2/gifti/issues
Encoding: UTF-8
LazyData: true
RoxygenNote: 7.1.2
VignetteBuilder: knitr

   154→    n = info$n[ind]

   156→    dat = data_decoder(
   157→      values = vals[[ind]],
   158→      encoding = encoding,
   159→      datatype = datatype,
   160→      endian = endian,

   162→      n = n)
   163→
   164→    namer = convert_intent(intent)
   165→    names(L)[ind] = namer
   166→
   167→    stopifnot(length(dat) == n)
   168→
   169→    mat_dims = info[ind, dims]

   171→    ord = info$ArrayIndexingOrder[ind]
   172→
   173→    arr = create_data_matrix(
   174→      data = dat,
   175→      dims = mat_dims,
   176→      ordering = ord)

   178→
   179→  }
   180→

   186→           parsed_transformations = parsed_trans,
   187→           label = lab_tab,
   188→           data_info = info
   189→  )
   190→  class(L) = "gifti"
   191→  return(L)
   192→}
   193→
   194→#' @rdname readgii

file.content:
#' @title Read GIFTI File
#' @description Reads a GIFTI File and parses the output
#'
#' @param file Name of file to read
#'
#' @return List of values
#' @export
#' @importFrom xml2 xml_find_all read_xml xml_attr as_list
#' @importFrom xml2 xml_children xml_text xml_name
#' @importFrom R.utils gunzip
#' @importFrom tools file_ext
#' @examples
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'    gii_files = download_gifti_data(outdir = NULL)
#'    gii_list = lapply(gii_files, readgii)
#'    surf_files = grep("white[.]surf[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
#'    surfs = lapply(surf_files, surf_triangles)
#'
#'    col_file = grep("white[.]shape[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
#'    cdata = readgii(col_file)
#'    cdata = cdata$data$shape
#'    mypal = grDevices::colorRampPalette(colors = c("blue", "black", "red"))
#'    n = 4
#'    breaks = quantile(cdata)
#'     ints = cut(cdata, include.lowest = TRUE, breaks = breaks)
#'     ints = as.integer(ints)
#'     stopifnot(!any(is.na(ints)))
#'     cols = mypal(n)[ints]
#'     cols = cols[surfs[[1]]$triangle]
#' }
#' \dontrun{
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'
#'  if (requireNamespace("rgl", quietly = TRUE)) {
#'     rgl::rgl.open()
#'     rgl::rgl.triangles(surfs[[1]]$pointset, color = cols)
#'     rgl::play3d(rgl::spin3d(), duration = 5)
#'  }
#' }
#' }
#'
readgii = function(file){

  if (length(file) > 1) {
    res = lapply(file, readgii)
    return(res)
  }
  dn = dirname(file)
  ######################
  # Allow to read gii.gz
  ######################
  file = decompress_gii(file)
  doc = read_xml(file)
  n_data_arrays = xml2::xml_attr(doc, "NumberOfDataArrays")
  n_data_arrays = as.numeric(n_data_arrays)
  ver = xml2::xml_attr(doc, "Version")

  ###################################
  # Parsing Meta Data
  ###################################
  meta = xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./MetaData")
  f = function(xpath) {
    xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(xml2::xml_children(meta), xpath))
  }
  meta_names = f("./Name")
  meta = f("./Value")
  names(meta) = meta_names

  lab_tab_pre = xml2::xml_children(xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./LabelTable"))
  lab_tab = do.call("rbind", xml2::xml_attrs(lab_tab_pre))
  if (!is.null(lab_tab)) {
    rownames(lab_tab) = xml2::xml_text(lab_tab_pre)
    }

  darray = xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./DataArray")
  # darray = as_list(darray)
  stopifnot(length(darray) == n_data_arrays)

  MD = lapply(darray, function(x) {
    xml2::xml_children(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./MetaData"))
  })
  MD = lapply(
    MD,
    function(x){
      names = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./Name"))
      vals = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./Value"))
      cbind(names = names,
            vals = vals)
    })

  # transformation matrix
  trans = lapply(
    darray,
    xml2::xml_find_all,
    xpath = "./CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")

  parsed_trans = lapply(trans, function(x) {
    cx = xml2::xml_children(x)
    n = xml2::xml_name(cx)
    res = lapply(cx, xml2::xml_text)
    names(res) = n
    if ("MatrixData" %in% n) {
      ind = n %in% "MatrixData"
      res[ind] = lapply(res[ind], function(mat){
        mat = strsplit(mat, "\n")[[1]]
        mat = trimws(mat)
        mat = mat[ !mat %in% ""]
        mat = lapply(strsplit(mat, " "), as.numeric)
        mat = do.call("rbind", mat)
        return(mat)
      })
    }
    return(res)
  })

  no_length_zero = function(x) {
    if (length(x) == 0) {
      return(NULL)
    } else {
      return(x)
    }
  }

  trans = lapply(trans, no_length_zero)
  parsed_trans = lapply(parsed_trans, no_length_zero)

  info = data_array_attributes(darray)
  dims = grep("^Dim\\d",
              colnames(info),
              value = TRUE)
  info$name = sapply(info$Intent, convert_intent)


  data = lapply(darray,
                xml2::xml_find_all,
                xpath = "./Data")
  vals = lapply(data,
                xml2::xml_text)

  ind = 1
  N = nrow(info)
  L = vector(mode = "list",
             length = N)

  for (ind in seq(N)) {

    encoding = info$Encoding[ind]
    datatype = info$DataType[ind]
    intent = info$Intent[ind]
    endian = info$Endian[ind]
    endian = convert_endian(endian)
    ext_filename = info$ExternalFileName[ind]
    ext_filename = file.path(dn, ext_filename)
    n = info$n[ind]

    dat = data_decoder(
      values = vals[[ind]],
      encoding = encoding,
      datatype = datatype,
      endian = endian,
      ext_filename = ext_filename,
      n = n)

    namer = convert_intent(intent)
    names(L)[ind] = namer

    stopifnot(length(dat) == n)

    mat_dims = info[ind, dims]
    mat_dims = unlist(mat_dims)
    ord = info$ArrayIndexingOrder[ind]

    arr = create_data_matrix(
      data = dat,
      dims = mat_dims,
      ordering = ord)
    L[[ind]] = arr

  }

  L = list(data = L,
           file_meta = meta,
           data_meta = MD,
           version = ver,
           transformations = trans,
           parsed_transformations = parsed_trans,
           label = lab_tab,
           data_info = info
  )
  class(L) = "gifti"
  return(L)
}

#' @rdname readgii
#' @export
readGIfTI = function(file){
  res = readgii(file)
  return(res)
}

#' @rdname readgii
#' @export
read_gifti = function(file){
  res = readgii(file)
  return(res)
}

content.content:
1→#' @title Write .gii xml from "gifti" object
     2→#' @description Writes a "gifti" object to a GIFTI file (ends in *.gii).
     3→#'
     4→#' @param gii The "gifti" object
     5→#' @param out_file Where to write the new GIFTI file
     6→#' @param use_parsed_transformations Should the \code{$parsed_transformations}
     7→#'  be written instead of the \code{transformations}? Use if the XML pointers
     8→#'  in \code{transformations} might be invalid. Default: \code{FALSE}
     9→#'
    10→#' @import xml2
    11→#' @export
    12→writegii <- function(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations=FALSE){
    13→  stopifnot(is.gifti(gii))
    14→
    15→  # GIFTI ROOT
    16→  root <- xml2::xml_add_child(xml2::xml_new_document(),
    17→    "GIFTI",
    18→    Version=gii$version,
    19→    `xmlns:xsi`="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance",
    20→    noNamespaceSchemaLocation="http://brainvis.wustl.edu/caret6/xml_schemas/GIFTI_Caret.xsd",
    21→    NumberOfDataArrays=length(gii$data)
    22→  )
    23→
    24→  # Can't get this to work.
    25→  # So, I add the doctype declaration at the end via gsub
    26→  #xml_add_child(root, as.character(xml_dtd("GIFTI" ,"SYSTEM", "\"http://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php/1594/gifti.dtd\">")))
    27→
    28→  # META DATA
    29→  file_meta <- xml2::xml_add_child(root, "MetaData")
    30→  if (length(gii$file_meta) > 0) {
    31→    for (ii in 1:length(gii$file_meta)) {
    32→      MD_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(file_meta, "MD")
    33→      N_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_ii, "Name")
    34→      xml2::xml_add_child(
    35→        N_ii,
    36→                          xml2::xml_cdata(attributes(gii$file_meta)$names[ii]))
    37→      V_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_ii, "Value")
    38→      xml2::xml_add_child(
    39→        V_ii,
    40→                          xml2::xml_cdata(as.character(gii$file_meta)[ii]))
    41→    }
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  # LABEL TABLE
    45→  labels <- xml2::xml_add_child(root, "LabelTable")
    46→  if (!is.null(gii$label)) {
    47→    for (ii in 1:nrow(gii$label)) {
    48→      label_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(
    49→        labels, "Label",
    50→        Key=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Key")],
    51→        Red=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Red")],
    52→        Green=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Green")],
    53→        Blue=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Blue")],
    54→        Alpha=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Alpha")]
    55→      )
    56→      xml2::xml_add_child(
    57→        label_ii, xml2::xml_cdata(rownames(gii$label)[ii]))
    58→    }
    59→  }
    60→
    61→  # DATA ARRAY
    62→  for (ii in 1:length(gii$data)) {
    63→    D_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(root, "DataArray")
    64→
    65→    # DataArray Attributes
    66→    for (jj in 1:ncol(gii$data_info)) {
    67→      atr_jj <- colnames(gii$data_info)[jj]
    68→      if (atr_jj %in% c("n", "name")) {next}
    69→      xml_attr(D_ii, atr_jj) <- gii$data_info[ii, atr_jj]
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    # DataArray MetaData
    73→    D_ii_meta <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii, "MetaData")
    74→    if (nrow(gii$data_meta[[ii]] > 0)) {
    75→      for (jj in 1:nrow(gii$data_meta[[ii]])) {
    76→        MD_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii_meta, "MD")
    77→        N_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_jj, "Name")
    78→        xml2::xml_add_child(
    79→          N_jj, xml2::xml_cdata(gii$data_meta[[ii]][jj,1]))
    80→        V_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_jj, "Value")
    81→        xml2::xml_add_child(V_jj,
    82→                            xml2::xml_cdata(gii$data_meta[[ii]][jj,2]))
    83→      }
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    # DataArray Transformations
    87→    ## From parsed
    88→    if (use_parsed_transformations && !is.null(gii$transformations[[ii]])){
    89→      for (jj in 1:(length(gii$parsed_transformations[[ii]])/3)) {
    90→        CSTM <- gii$parsed_transformations[[ii]][(jj-1)*3 + 1:3]
    91→        T_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii, "CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")
    92→        T_D_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(T_jj, "DataSpace")
    93→        xml2::xml_add_child(T_D_jj,
    94→                            xml2::xml_cdata(CSTM[[1]]))
    95→        T_D_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(T_jj, "TransformedSpace")
    96→        xml2::xml_add_child(T_D_jj, xml2::xml_cdata(CSTM[[2]]))
    97→        T_D_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(
    98→          T_jj, "MatrixData",
    99→          paste(apply(CSTM[[3]], 1, paste, collapse=" "), collapse="\n")
   100→        )
   101→      }
   102→    ## From not parsed
   103→    } else {
   104→      for (jj in 1:length(gii$transformations[[ii]])) {
   105→        CSTM <- gii$transformations[[ii]][[jj]]
   106→        if (is.null(CSTM)) {next}
   107→        T_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii, "CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")
   108→        xml2::xml_replace(T_jj, CSTM)
   109→      }
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    # DataArray Data
   113→    # [TO DO]: external files?
   114→    # [TO DO]: resolve below case
   115→    if (gii$data_info$Encoding[ii] != "ASCII" &&
   116→        gii$data_info$DataType[ii] == "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32") {
   117→      stop("Not working right now: NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 and non-ASCII encoding.")
   118→    }
   119→    dat <- gii$data[[ii]]
   120→    if ((length(dim(dat)) > 1) &&
   121→        gii$data_info$ArrayIndexingOrder[ii]=="RowMajorOrder") {
   122→      dat <- aperm(dat, length(dim(dat)):1)
   123→    }
   124→    D_ii_data <- xml2::xml_add_child(
   125→      D_ii,
   126→      "Data",
   127→      data_encoder(
   128→        as.numeric(dat),
   129→        encoding = gii$data_info$Encoding[ii],
   130→        datatype = gii$data_info$DataType[ii],
   131→        endian = gii$data_info$Endian[ii]
   132→      )
   133→    )
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  # Add doctag and write it.
   137→  to_write <- as.character(root)
   138→  to_write <- gsub("<GIFTI", '<!DOCTYPE GIFTI SYSTEM "http://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php/1594/gifti.dtd">
   139→  <GIFTI', to_write, fixed=TRUE)
   140→  writeLines(to_write, out_file)
   141→}
   142→
   143→#' @rdname writegii
   144→#' @export
   145→writeGIfTI = function(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations=FALSE){
   146→  writegii(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations)
   147→}
   148→
   149→#' @rdname writegii
   150→#' @export
   151→write_gifti = function(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations=FALSE){
   152→  writegii(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations)
   153→}
   154→

I need to analyze the R gifti package structure and create a comprehensive list of all functions that need to be ported to Rust. Please examine the /Users/bbuchsbaum/code/rust/gifti-rs/reference-gifti/R/ directory and:

1. List all .R files and their purposes
2. Identify the core functions exported in NAMESPACE
3. Analyze the complexity and dependencies of each function
4. Create a priority ranking for implementation
5. Identify any R-specific features that need special attention in Rust (S3 methods, etc.)
6. Look for any C/C++ FFI calls or performance-critical sections
7. Examine the test files to understand expected behavior

Focus on understanding the complete API surface and implementation strategy needed for the Rust port.

file.content:
Package: gifti
Type: Package
Title: Reads in 'Neuroimaging' 'GIFTI' Files with Geometry Information
Version: 0.8.2
Date: 2021-03-31
Author: John Muschelli
Maintainer: John Muschelli <muschellij2@gmail.com>
Description: Functions to read in the geometry format under the 
    'Neuroimaging' 'Informatics' Technology Initiative ('NIfTI'), called 
    'GIFTI' <https://www.nitrc.org/projects/gifti/>. 
    These files contain surfaces of brain imaging data.
License: GPL-2
Imports:
    xml2 (>= 1.1.1),
    base64enc,
    R.utils,
    tools,
    utils
Suggests:
    rgl,
    grDevices,
    testthat,
    knitr,
    rmarkdown,
    covr
BugReports: https://github.com/muschellij2/gifti/issues
Encoding: UTF-8
LazyData: true
RoxygenNote: 7.1.2
VignetteBuilder: knitr

    42→readgii = function(file){
    43→
    44→  if (length(file) > 1) {
    45→    res = lapply(file, readgii)
    46→    return(res)
    47→  }
    48→  dn = dirname(file)
    49→  ######################
    50→  # Allow to read gii.gz
    51→  ######################
    52→  file = decompress_gii(file)
    53→  doc = read_xml(file)
    54→  n_data_arrays = xml2::xml_attr(doc, "NumberOfDataArrays")

    56→  ver = xml2::xml_attr(doc, "Version")

    58→  ###################################

    61→  meta = xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./MetaData")
    62→  f = function(xpath) {
    63→    xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(xml2::xml_children(meta), xpath))
    64→  }
    65→  meta_names = f("./Name")
    66→  meta = f("./Value")
    67→  names(meta) = meta_names

    69→  lab_tab_pre = xml2::xml_children(xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./LabelTable"))
    70→  lab_tab = do.call("rbind", xml2::xml_attrs(lab_tab_pre))
    71→  if (!is.null(lab_tab)) {
    72→    rownames(lab_tab) = xml2::xml_text(lab_tab_pre)
    73→    }
    74→
    75→  darray = xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./DataArray")
    76→  # darray = as_list(darray)
    77→  stopifnot(length(darray) == n_data_arrays)
    78→
    79→  MD = lapply(darray, function(x) {
    80→    xml2::xml_children(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./MetaData"))
    81→  })
    82→  MD = lapply(
    83→    MD,
    84→    function(x){
    85→      names = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./Name"))
    86→      vals = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./Value"))
    87→      cbind(names = names,
    88→            vals = vals)
    89→    })
    90→
    91→  # transformation matrix
    92→  trans = lapply(
    93→    darray,
    94→    xml2::xml_find_all,
    95→    xpath = "./CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")
    96→
    97→  parsed_trans = lapply(trans, function(x) {
    98→    cx = xml2::xml_children(x)
    99→    n = xml2::xml_name(cx)
   100→    res = lapply(cx, xml2::xml_text)
   101→    names(res) = n
   102→    if ("MatrixData" %in% n) {
   103→      ind = n %in% "MatrixData"
   104→      res[ind] = lapply(res[ind], function(mat){
   105→        mat = strsplit(mat, "\n")[[1]]
   106→        mat = trimws(mat)
   107→        mat = mat[ !mat %in% ""]
   108→        mat = lapply(strsplit(mat, " "), as.numeric)
   109→        mat = do.call("rbind", mat)
   110→        return(mat)
   111→      })
   112→    }
   113→    return(res)
   114→  })
   115→
   116→  no_length_zero = function(x) {
   117→    if (length(x) == 0) {
   118→      return(NULL)
   119→    } else {
   120→      return(x)
   121→    }
   122→  }
   123→
   124→  trans = lapply(trans, no_length_zero)
   125→  parsed_trans = lapply(parsed_trans, no_length_zero)
   126→
   127→  info = data_array_attributes(darray)
   128→  dims = grep("^Dim\\d",
   129→              colnames(info),
   130→              value = TRUE)
   131→  info$name = sapply(info$Intent, convert_intent)
   132→
   133→
   134→  data = lapply(darray,
   135→                xml2::xml_find_all,
   136→                xpath = "./Data")
   137→  vals = lapply(data,
   138→                xml2::xml_text)
   139→
   140→  ind = 1
   141→  N = nrow(info)
   142→  L = vector(mode = "list",
   143→             length = N)
   144→
   145→  for (ind in seq(N)) {
   146→
   147→    encoding = info$Encoding[ind]
   148→    datatype = info$DataType[ind]
   149→    intent = info$Intent[ind]
   150→    endian = info$Endian[ind]
   151→    endian = convert_endian(endian)
   152→    ext_filename = info$ExternalFileName[ind]
   153→    ext_filename = file.path(dn, ext_filename)
   154→    n = info$n[ind]
   155→
   156→    dat = data_decoder(
   157→      values = vals[[ind]],
   158→      encoding = encoding,
   159→      datatype = datatype,
   160→      endian = endian,
   161→      ext_filename = ext_filename,
   162→      n = n)
   163→
   164→    namer = convert_intent(intent)
   165→    names(L)[ind] = namer
   166→
   167→    stopifnot(length(dat) == n)
   168→
   169→    mat_dims = info[ind, dims]
   170→    mat_dims = unlist(mat_dims)
   171→    ord = info$ArrayIndexingOrder[ind]
   172→
   173→    arr = create_data_matrix(
   174→      data = dat,
   175→      dims = mat_dims,
   176→      ordering = ord)
   177→    L[[ind]] = arr
   178→
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  L = list(data = L,
   182→           file_meta = meta,
   183→           data_meta = MD,
   184→           version = ver,
   185→           transformations = trans,
   186→           parsed_transformations = parsed_trans,
   187→           label = lab_tab,
   188→           data_info = info
   189→  )
   190→  class(L) = "gifti"
   191→  return(L)
   192→}
   193→
   194→#' @rdname readgii
   195→#' @export
   196→readGIfTI = function(file){
   197→  res = readgii(file)
   198→  return(res)
   199→}
   200→
   201→#' @rdname readgii
   202→#' @export
   203→read_gifti = function(file){
   204→  res = readgii(file)
   205→  return(res)
   206→}
   207→

file.content:
#' @title Read GIFTI File
#' @description Reads a GIFTI File and parses the output
#'
#' @param file Name of file to read
#'
#' @return List of values
#' @export
#' @importFrom xml2 xml_find_all read_xml xml_attr as_list
#' @importFrom xml2 xml_children xml_text xml_name
#' @importFrom R.utils gunzip
#' @importFrom tools file_ext
#' @examples
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'    gii_files = download_gifti_data(outdir = NULL)
#'    gii_list = lapply(gii_files, readgii)
#'    surf_files = grep("white[.]surf[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
#'    surfs = lapply(surf_files, surf_triangles)
#'
#'    col_file = grep("white[.]shape[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
#'    cdata = readgii(col_file)
#'    cdata = cdata$data$shape
#'    mypal = grDevices::colorRampPalette(colors = c("blue", "black", "red"))
#'    n = 4
#'    breaks = quantile(cdata)
#'     ints = cut(cdata, include.lowest = TRUE, breaks = breaks)
#'     ints = as.integer(ints)
#'     stopifnot(!any(is.na(ints)))
#'     cols = mypal(n)[ints]
#'     cols = cols[surfs[[1]]$triangle]
#' }
#' \dontrun{
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'
#'  if (requireNamespace("rgl", quietly = TRUE)) {
#'     rgl::rgl.open()
#'     rgl::rgl.triangles(surfs[[1]]$pointset, color = cols)
#'     rgl::play3d(rgl::spin3d(), duration = 5)
#'  }
#' }
#' }
#'
readgii = function(file){

  if (length(file) > 1) {
    res = lapply(file, readgii)
    return(res)
  }
  dn = dirname(file)
  ######################
  # Allow to read gii.gz
  ######################
  file = decompress_gii(file)
  doc = read_xml(file)
  n_data_arrays = xml2::xml_attr(doc, "NumberOfDataArrays")
  n_data_arrays = as.numeric(n_data_arrays)
  ver = xml2::xml_attr(doc, "Version")

  ###################################
  # Parsing Meta Data
  ###################################
  meta = xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./MetaData")
  f = function(xpath) {
    xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(xml2::xml_children(meta), xpath))
  }
  meta_names = f("./Name")
  meta = f("./Value")
  names(meta) = meta_names

  lab_tab_pre = xml2::xml_children(xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./LabelTable"))
  lab_tab = do.call("rbind", xml2::xml_attrs(lab_tab_pre))
  if (!is.null(lab_tab)) {
    rownames(lab_tab) = xml2::xml_text(lab_tab_pre)
    }

  darray = xml2::xml_find_all(doc, "./DataArray")
  # darray = as_list(darray)
  stopifnot(length(darray) == n_data_arrays)

  MD = lapply(darray, function(x) {
    xml2::xml_children(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./MetaData"))
  })
  MD = lapply(
    MD,
    function(x){
      names = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./Name"))
      vals = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_all(x, "./Value"))
      cbind(names = names,
            vals = vals)
    })

  # transformation matrix
  trans = lapply(
    darray,
    xml2::xml_find_all,
    xpath = "./CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")

  parsed_trans = lapply(trans, function(x) {
    cx = xml2::xml_children(x)
    n = xml2::xml_name(cx)
    res = lapply(cx, xml2::xml_text)
    names(res) = n
    if ("MatrixData" %in% n) {
      ind = n %in% "MatrixData"
      res[ind] = lapply(res[ind], function(mat){
        mat = strsplit(mat, "\n")[[1]]
        mat = trimws(mat)
        mat = mat[ !mat %in% ""]
        mat = lapply(strsplit(mat, " "), as.numeric)
        mat = do.call("rbind", mat)
        return(mat)
      })
    }
    return(res)
  })

  no_length_zero = function(x) {
    if (length(x) == 0) {
      return(NULL)
    } else {
      return(x)
    }
  }

  trans = lapply(trans, no_length_zero)
  parsed_trans = lapply(parsed_trans, no_length_zero)

  info = data_array_attributes(darray)
  dims = grep("^Dim\\d",
              colnames(info),
              value = TRUE)
  info$name = sapply(info$Intent, convert_intent)


  data = lapply(darray,
                xml2::xml_find_all,
                xpath = "./Data")
  vals = lapply(data,
                xml2::xml_text)

  ind = 1
  N = nrow(info)
  L = vector(mode = "list",
             length = N)

  for (ind in seq(N)) {

    encoding = info$Encoding[ind]
    datatype = info$DataType[ind]
    intent = info$Intent[ind]
    endian = info$Endian[ind]
    endian = convert_endian(endian)
    ext_filename = info$ExternalFileName[ind]
    ext_filename = file.path(dn, ext_filename)
    n = info$n[ind]

    dat = data_decoder(
      values = vals[[ind]],
      encoding = encoding,
      datatype = datatype,
      endian = endian,
      ext_filename = ext_filename,
      n = n)

    namer = convert_intent(intent)
    names(L)[ind] = namer

    stopifnot(length(dat) == n)

    mat_dims = info[ind, dims]
    mat_dims = unlist(mat_dims)
    ord = info$ArrayIndexingOrder[ind]

    arr = create_data_matrix(
      data = dat,
      dims = mat_dims,
      ordering = ord)
    L[[ind]] = arr

  }

  L = list(data = L,
           file_meta = meta,
           data_meta = MD,
           version = ver,
           transformations = trans,
           parsed_transformations = parsed_trans,
           label = lab_tab,
           data_info = info
  )
  class(L) = "gifti"
  return(L)
}

#' @rdname readgii
#' @export
readGIfTI = function(file){
  res = readgii(file)
  return(res)
}

#' @rdname readgii
#' @export
read_gifti = function(file){
  res = readgii(file)
  return(res)
}

1→#' @title Write .gii xml from "gifti" object
     2→#' @description Writes a "gifti" object to a GIFTI file (ends in *.gii).
     3→#'
     4→#' @param gii The "gifti" object
     5→#' @param out_file Where to write the new GIFTI file
     6→#' @param use_parsed_transformations Should the \code{$parsed_transformations}
     7→#'  be written instead of the \code{transformations}? Use if the XML pointers
     8→#'  in \code{transformations} might be invalid. Default: \code{FALSE}
     9→#'
    10→#' @import xml2
    11→#' @export
    12→writegii <- function(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations=FALSE){
    13→  stopifnot(is.gifti(gii))
    14→
    15→  # GIFTI ROOT
    16→  root <- xml2::xml_add_child(xml2::xml_new_document(),
    17→    "GIFTI",
    18→    Version=gii$version,
    19→    `xmlns:xsi`="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance",
    20→    noNamespaceSchemaLocation="http://brainvis.wustl.edu/caret6/xml_schemas/GIFTI_Caret.xsd",
    21→    NumberOfDataArrays=length(gii$data)
    22→  )
    23→
    24→  # Can't get this to work.
    25→  # So, I add the doctype declaration at the end via gsub
    26→  #xml_add_child(root, as.character(xml_dtd("GIFTI" ,"SYSTEM", "\"http://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php/1594/gifti.dtd\">")))
    27→
    28→  # META DATA
    29→  file_meta <- xml2::xml_add_child(root, "MetaData")
    30→  if (length(gii$file_meta) > 0) {
    31→    for (ii in 1:length(gii$file_meta)) {
    32→      MD_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(file_meta, "MD")
    33→      N_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_ii, "Name")
    34→      xml2::xml_add_child(
    35→        N_ii,
    36→                          xml2::xml_cdata(attributes(gii$file_meta)$names[ii]))
    37→      V_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_ii, "Value")
    38→      xml2::xml_add_child(
    39→        V_ii,
    40→                          xml2::xml_cdata(as.character(gii$file_meta)[ii]))
    41→    }
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  # LABEL TABLE
    45→  labels <- xml2::xml_add_child(root, "LabelTable")
    46→  if (!is.null(gii$label)) {
    47→    for (ii in 1:nrow(gii$label)) {
    48→      label_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(
    49→        labels, "Label",
    50→        Key=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Key")],
    51→        Red=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Red")],
    52→        Green=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Green")],
    53→        Blue=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Blue")],
    54→        Alpha=gii$label[ii,which(colnames(gii$label)=="Alpha")]
    55→      )
    56→      xml2::xml_add_child(
    57→        label_ii, xml2::xml_cdata(rownames(gii$label)[ii]))
    58→    }
    59→  }
    60→
    61→  # DATA ARRAY
    62→  for (ii in 1:length(gii$data)) {
    63→    D_ii <- xml2::xml_add_child(root, "DataArray")
    64→
    65→    # DataArray Attributes
    66→    for (jj in 1:ncol(gii$data_info)) {
    67→      atr_jj <- colnames(gii$data_info)[jj]
    68→      if (atr_jj %in% c("n", "name")) {next}
    69→      xml_attr(D_ii, atr_jj) <- gii$data_info[ii, atr_jj]
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    # DataArray MetaData
    73→    D_ii_meta <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii, "MetaData")
    74→    if (nrow(gii$data_meta[[ii]] > 0)) {
    75→      for (jj in 1:nrow(gii$data_meta[[ii]])) {
    76→        MD_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii_meta, "MD")
    77→        N_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_jj, "Name")
    78→        xml2::xml_add_child(
    79→          N_jj, xml2::xml_cdata(gii$data_meta[[ii]][jj,1]))
    80→        V_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(MD_jj, "Value")
    81→        xml2::xml_add_child(V_jj,
    82→                            xml2::xml_cdata(gii$data_meta[[ii]][jj,2]))
    83→      }
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    # DataArray Transformations
    87→    ## From parsed
    88→    if (use_parsed_transformations && !is.null(gii$transformations[[ii]])){
    89→      for (jj in 1:(length(gii$parsed_transformations[[ii]])/3)) {
    90→        CSTM <- gii$parsed_transformations[[ii]][(jj-1)*3 + 1:3]
    91→        T_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii, "CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")
    92→        T_D_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(T_jj, "DataSpace")
    93→        xml2::xml_add_child(T_D_jj,
    94→                            xml2::xml_cdata(CSTM[[1]]))
    95→        T_D_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(T_jj, "TransformedSpace")
    96→        xml2::xml_add_child(T_D_jj, xml2::xml_cdata(CSTM[[2]]))
    97→        T_D_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(
    98→          T_jj, "MatrixData",
    99→          paste(apply(CSTM[[3]], 1, paste, collapse=" "), collapse="\n")
   100→        )
   101→      }
   102→    ## From not parsed
   103→    } else {
   104→      for (jj in 1:length(gii$transformations[[ii]])) {
   105→        CSTM <- gii$transformations[[ii]][[jj]]
   106→        if (is.null(CSTM)) {next}
   107→        T_jj <- xml2::xml_add_child(D_ii, "CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix")
   108→        xml2::xml_replace(T_jj, CSTM)
   109→      }
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    # DataArray Data
   113→    # [TO DO]: external files?
   114→    # [TO DO]: resolve below case
   115→    if (gii$data_info$Encoding[ii] != "ASCII" &&
   116→        gii$data_info$DataType[ii] == "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32") {
   117→      stop("Not working right now: NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 and non-ASCII encoding.")
   118→    }
   119→    dat <- gii$data[[ii]]
   120→    if ((length(dim(dat)) > 1) &&
   121→        gii$data_info$ArrayIndexingOrder[ii]=="RowMajorOrder") {
   122→      dat <- aperm(dat, length(dim(dat)):1)
   123→    }
   124→    D_ii_data <- xml2::xml_add_child(
   125→      D_ii,
   126→      "Data",
   127→      data_encoder(
   128→        as.numeric(dat),
   129→        encoding = gii$data_info$Encoding[ii],
   130→        datatype = gii$data_info$DataType[ii],
   131→        endian = gii$data_info$Endian[ii]
   132→      )
   133→    )
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  # Add doctag and write it.
   137→  to_write <- as.character(root)
   138→  to_write <- gsub("<GIFTI", '<!DOCTYPE GIFTI SYSTEM "http://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php/1594/gifti.dtd">
   139→  <GIFTI', to_write, fixed=TRUE)
   140→  writeLines(to_write, out_file)
   141→}
   142→
   143→#' @rdname writegii
   144→#' @export
   145→writeGIfTI = function(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations=FALSE){
   146→  writegii(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations)
   147→}
   148→
   149→#' @rdname writegii
   150→#' @export
   151→write_gifti = function(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations=FALSE){
   152→  writegii(gii, out_file, use_parsed_transformations)
   153→}
   154→

#' @title Array Data Decoder
#' @description Decodes values from a GIFTI image
#'
#' @param values text from XML of GIFTI image
#' @param encoding encoding of GIFTI values
#' @param datatype Passed to \code{\link{convert_binary_datatype}}
#' @param endian Endian to pass in \code{\link{readBin}}
#' @param ext_filename if \code{encoding = "ExternalFileBinary"}, then
#' this is the external filename
#' @param n number of values to read.  Relevant if
#' \code{encoding = "ExternalFileBinary"}
#'
#' @return Vector of values
#' @export
#'
#' @importFrom base64enc base64decode
#' @examples
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'    gii_files = download_gifti_data(outdir = NULL)
#'    L = gifti_list(gii_files[1])
#'    orig = L$DataArray$Data[[1]]
#'    encoding = attributes(L$DataArray)$Encoding
#'    datatype = attributes(L$DataArray)$DataType
#'    endian = attributes(L$DataArray)$Endian
#'    vals =  data_decoder(orig, encoding = encoding,
#'    datatype = datatype, endian = endian)
#'    enc = data_encoder(vals, encoding = encoding,
#'    datatype = datatype, endian = endian)
#'    enc == orig
#' }
data_decoder = function(
  values,
  encoding = c("ASCII",
               "Base64Binary",
               "GZipBase64Binary",
               "ExternalFileBinary"),
  datatype = NULL,
  endian = c("little", "big",
             "LittleEndian", "BigEndian"),
  ext_filename = NULL,
  n = NULL) {

  encoding = match.arg(encoding)
  if (encoding == "ExternalFileBinary") {
    msg = paste0(
      "Encoding says ExternalFileBinary, but file ",
      "doesn't exist in",
      " the same directory as the gii.  File was ",
      ext_filename)
    if (is.null(ext_filename)) {
      stop( msg)
    }
    if (!file.exists(ext_filename)) {
      stop( msg)
    }
    values = ext_filename
  }
  if (encoding == "ASCII") {
    values = strsplit(values, "\n")
    values = values[[1]]
    values = values[ !values %in% "" ]
    values = trimws(values)
    values = strsplit(values, " ")
    values = lapply(values, as.numeric)
    values = unlist(values)
    return(values)
  }
  if (grepl("Base64Binary", encoding)) {
    values = base64enc::base64decode(values)
    n = length(values) * 2
  }
  if (grepl("GZip", encoding)) {
    values = memDecompress(values, type = "gzip")
    n = length(values) * 2
  }

  L = convert_binary_datatype(datatype = datatype)
  size = L$size
  what = L$what
  endian = match.arg(endian)
  endian = tolower(endian)
  endian = gsub("endian$", "", endian)
  values = readBin(
    con = values,
    what = what,
    size = size,
    endian = endian,
    n = n
  )

  return(values)
}

#' @title Array Data Encoder
#' @description Encodes values for a GIFTI image
#'
#' @param values values to be encoded
#' @param encoding encoding of GIFTI values
#' @param datatype Passed to \code{\link{convert_binary_datatype}}
#' @param endian Endian to pass in \code{\link{readBin}}
#'
#' @return Single character vector
#' @export
#'
#' @importFrom base64enc base64encode
#' @examples
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'    gii_files = download_gifti_data(outdir = NULL)
#'    L = gifti_list(gii_files[1])
#'    orig = L$DataArray$Data[[1]]
#'    encoding = attributes(L$DataArray)$Encoding
#'    datatype = attributes(L$DataArray)$DataType
#'    endian = attributes(L$DataArray)$Endian
#'    vals =  data_decoder(orig, encoding = encoding,
#'    datatype = datatype, endian = endian)
#'    enc = data_encoder(vals, encoding = encoding,
#'    datatype = datatype, endian = endian)
#'    enc == orig
#' }
data_encoder = function(
  values,
  encoding = c("ASCII",
               "Base64Binary",
               "GZipBase64Binary"),
  datatype = NULL,
  endian = c("little", "big",
             "LittleEndian", "BigEndian")) {

  encoding = match.arg(encoding)
  if (encoding == "ASCII") {
    values = format(values, scientific=FALSE)
    values = paste(values, collapse="\n")
    return(values)
  }
  L = convert_binary_datatype(datatype = datatype)
  size = L$size
  # what = L$what
  endian = match.arg(endian)
  endian = tolower(endian)
  endian = gsub("endian$", "", endian)
  values = writeBin(object = values,
                   con = raw(),
                   size = size,
                   endian = endian
  )
  if (grepl("GZip", encoding)) {
    values = memCompress(values, type = "gzip")
  }
  if (grepl("Base64Binary", encoding)) {
    values = base64enc::base64encode(values)
  }
  return(values)
}

1→#' @title Convert Binary Data Type
     2→#' @description Converts a data type to the size and \code{what} for
     3→#' \code{\link{readBin}}, necessary for \code{Base64Binary} and
     4→#' \code{GZipBase64Binary} formats
     5→#'
     6→#' @param datatype data type from GIFTI image
     7→#'
     8→#' @return List of length 2: with elements of size and what
     9→#' @export
    10→#' @examples
    11→#' convert_binary_datatype()
    12→#' convert_binary_datatype('NIFTI_TYPE_INT32')
    13→#' testthat::expect_error(convert_binary_datatype('NIFTI_TYPE_BLAH'))
    14→convert_binary_datatype = function(
    15→  datatype = c("NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8",
    16→               "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32",
    17→               "NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32", # this exists but is bad
    18→               "NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32")){
    19→  in_data = datatype
    20→  res = try({
    21→    datatype = match.arg(datatype)
    22→  }, silent = TRUE)
    23→  if (inherits(res, "try-error")) {
    24→    stop(paste0("GIFTI datatype is unknown; ",
    25→           "datatype is ", in_data))
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  if (datatype %in% c("NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32")) {
    29→    warning(paste0("datatype given is not specified in GIFTI DTD, ",
    30→                   "results may be not correct!"))
    31→  }
    32→  size = switch(datatype,
    33→                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8 = 1,
    34→                NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 = 4,
    35→                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32 = 4,
    36→                NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32 = 4)
    37→  what = switch(datatype,
    38→                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8 = integer(),
    39→                NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 = integer(),
    40→                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32 = integer(),
    41→                NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32 = double())
    42→  L = list(size = size, what = what)
    43→  return(L)
    44→}

#' @title Convert Binary Data Type
#' @description Converts a data type to the size and \code{what} for
#' \code{\link{readBin}}, necessary for \code{Base64Binary} and
#' \code{GZipBase64Binary} formats
#'
#' @param datatype data type from GIFTI image
#'
#' @return List of length 2: with elements of size and what
#' @export
#' @examples
#' convert_binary_datatype()
#' convert_binary_datatype('NIFTI_TYPE_INT32')
#' testthat::expect_error(convert_binary_datatype('NIFTI_TYPE_BLAH'))
convert_binary_datatype = function(
  datatype = c("NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8",
               "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32",
               "NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32", # this exists but is bad
               "NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32")){
  in_data = datatype
  res = try({
    datatype = match.arg(datatype)
  }, silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(res, "try-error")) {
    stop(paste0("GIFTI datatype is unknown; ",
           "datatype is ", in_data))
  }

  if (datatype %in% c("NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32")) {
    warning(paste0("datatype given is not specified in GIFTI DTD, ",
                   "results may be not correct!"))
  }
  size = switch(datatype,
                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8 = 1,
                NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 = 4,
                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32 = 4,
                NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32 = 4)
  what = switch(datatype,
                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8 = integer(),
                NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 = integer(),
                NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32 = integer(),
                NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32 = double())
  L = list(size = size, what = what)
  return(L)
}

#' @title Convert Endian from GIFTI

#'
#' @param endian character passed from GIFTI XML
#'

convert_endian = function(endian) {
  endian = gsub("Endian", "", endian)
  endian = tolower(endian)
  return(endian)
}

#' @title Data Array Attributes
#' @description Parses a list of XML data to get the attributes
#'
#' @param darray List of \code{xml_nodes} from GIFTI data array
#'
#' @return \code{data.frame} of attributes
#' @export
#' @importFrom xml2 xml_attrs
data_array_attributes = function(darray) {
  ################################################
  # Get external header information
  ################################################
  info = lapply(darray, xml_attrs)
  n1 = names(info[[1]])
  check = sapply(n1, function(x) {
    all(names(x) == n1)
  })
  if (!all(check)) {
    stop("Different named elements for each data array!")
  }
  info = do.call("rbind", info)
  info = data.frame(info, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  ################################################
  # Getting dimensions
  ################################################
  n1 = colnames(info)
  dims = grep("^Dim\\d", n1, value = TRUE)
  for (icol in dims) {
    info[, icol] = as.numeric(info[, icol])
  }
  info$n = apply(info[, dims, drop = FALSE], 1, prod)
  return(info)
}

1→#' Create Data Matrix with ordering respected
     2→#'
     3→#' @param data Data output from \code{\link{data_decoder}}
     4→#' @param dims Dimensions of output
     5→#' @param ordering Ordering of the data
     6→#'
     7→#' @return Matrix of Values
     8→#' @export
     9→create_data_matrix = function(
    10→  data,
    11→  dims,
    12→  ordering =  c("RowMajorOrder",
    13→                "ColumnMajorOrder")) {
    14→  ordering = match.arg(ordering)
    15→
    16→  ndims = length(dims)
    17→
    18→  byrow = ordering %in% "RowMajorOrder"
    19→
    20→
    21→  if (ndims == 1) {
    22→    data = matrix(data,
    23→                  nrow = dims[1],
    24→                  ncol = 1)
    25→  } else if (ndims == 2) {
    26→    data = matrix(data,
    27→                  nrow = dims[1],
    28→                  ncol = dims[2], byrow = byrow)
    29→  } else {
    30→    stop("not implemented")
    31→  }
    32→
    33→  return(data)
    34→}

#' @title Decompress Gzipped GIFTI (with extension .gz)
#' @description If a GIFTI file is compressed, as in .gii.gz, this will decompress
#' the file.  This has nothing to do with the encoding WITHIN the file
#'
#' @param file file name of GIFTI file
#'
#' @return Filename of decompressed GIFTI
#' @export
#' @examples
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'    gii_files = download_gifti_data(outdir = NULL)
#'    outfile = decompress_gii(gii_files[1])
#'    print(outfile)
#' }
decompress_gii = function(file) {
  ext = tolower(tools::file_ext(file))
  if (ext == "gz") {
    # destfile = tempfile(fileext = ".gii")
    file = R.utils::gunzip(file,
                           # destname = destfile,
                           temporary = TRUE,
                           remove = FALSE,
                           overwrite = TRUE)
  }
  return(file)
}

#' @title Map Values to Triangles from GIFTI
#' @description Takes values and maps them to the correct triangles in
#' space.
#'
#' @param pointset pointset from GIFTI
#' @param triangle triangles from GIFTI
#' @param values Values to map to the triangles.  Same length as indices
#' @param indices indices to place the values, must be in the range of 1 and
#' the number of rows of \code{pointset}
#' @param add_one Should \code{1} be added to the indices for the triangle?
#'
#' @return A list of coordinates (in triangles) and the corresponding value
#' mapped to those triangles
#' @export
gifti_map_value = function(
  pointset,
  triangle,
  values,
  indices = seq(nrow(pointset)),
  add_one = TRUE) {
  stopifnot(length(values) == length(indices))
  if (any(indices < 1)) {
    stop("Indices have numbers less than zero!")
  }

  dat = rep(NA, length = nrow(pointset))
  dat[ indices ] = values

  tri = as.vector(t(triangle) + add_one)
  dat = dat[tri]

  pointset = pointset[tri,]
  L = list(coords = pointset,
           values = dat)
}

#' @title Convert GIFTI to List
#' @description Reads in a GIFTI file and coerces it to a list
#'
#' @param file file name of GIFTI file
#'
#' @return List of elements
#' @export
#' @examples
#' if (have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)) {
#'    gii_files = download_gifti_data(outdir = NULL)
#'    L = gifti_list(gii_files[1])
#'    orig = L$DataArray$Data[[1]]
#'    encoding = attributes(L$DataArray)$Encoding
#'    datatype = attributes(L$DataArray)$DataType
#'    endian = attributes(L$DataArray)$Endian
#'    vals =  data_decoder(orig, encoding = encoding,
#'    datatype = datatype, endian = endian)
#'    enc = data_encoder(vals, encoding = encoding,
#'    datatype = datatype, endian = endian)
#'    enc == orig
#' }
gifti_list = function(file) {
  ######################
  # Allow to read gii.gz
  ######################
  file = decompress_gii(file)
  doc = read_xml(file)
  doc = as_list(doc)
  class(doc) = "gifti_list"
  return(doc)
}

1→#' @title Download GIFTI Test Data
     2→#' @description Downloads GIFTI test data from
     3→#' \url{https://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php/411/BV_GIFTI_1.3.tar.gz}
     4→#' @param outdir Output directory for test file directory
     5→#' @param overwrite Should files be overwritten if already exist?
     6→#' @param ... additional arguments to \code{\link{download.file}}
     7→#'
     8→#' @return Vector of file names
     9→#' @export
    10→#'
    11→#' @importFrom utils download.file untar
    12→#' @importFrom R.utils gzip
    13→download_gifti_data = function(
    14→  outdir = system.file(package = "gifti"),
    15→  overwrite = FALSE,
    16→  ...
    17→) {
    18→  # simple workaround for vignettes
    19→  if (is.null(outdir)) {
    20→    outdir = ""
    21→  }
    22→  if (outdir == "") {
    23→    outdir = tempdir()
    24→  }
    25→  expected_files = c("sujet01_Lwhite.shape.gii.gz",
    26→                     "fmri_sujet01_Lwhite_projection.time.gii.gz",
    27→                     "sujet01_Lwhite.surf.gii.gz",
    28→                     "sujet01_Lwhite.inflated.surf.gii.gz"
    29→  )
    30→  expected_files = file.path("BV_GIFTI", "GzipBase64", expected_files)
    31→  out_files = file.path(outdir, expected_files)
    32→  if (!all(file.exists(out_files)) || overwrite) {
    33→    # url = paste0( "http://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php",
    34→    #               "/411/BV_GIFTI_1.3.tar.gz")
    35→    url = "http://johnmuschelli.com/gifti/BV_GIFTI.tar.gz"
    36→
    37→    destfile = basename(url)
    38→    destfile = file.path(outdir, destfile)
    39→    download.file( url = url, destfile = destfile)
    40→
    41→    efiles = gsub("[.]gz$", "", expected_files)
    42→    untar(tarfile = destfile,
    43→          exdir = outdir,
    44→          files = efiles)
    45→    # compress them
    46→    files_to_zip = file.path(outdir, efiles)
    47→    sapply(files_to_zip, R.utils::gzip, compression = 9)
    48→    file.remove(destfile)
    49→  }
    50→  return(out_files)
    51→}

#' @title Download GIFTI Test Data
#' @description Downloads GIFTI test data from
#' \url{https://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php/411/BV_GIFTI_1.3.tar.gz}
#' @param outdir Output directory for test file directory
#' @param overwrite Should files be overwritten if already exist?
#' @param ... additional arguments to \code{\link{download.file}}
#'
#' @return Vector of file names
#' @export
#'
#' @importFrom utils download.file untar
#' @importFrom R.utils gzip
download_gifti_data = function(
  outdir = system.file(package = "gifti"),
  overwrite = FALSE,
  ...
) {
  # simple workaround for vignettes
  if (is.null(outdir)) {
    outdir = ""
  }
  if (outdir == "") {
    outdir = tempdir()
  }
  expected_files = c("sujet01_Lwhite.shape.gii.gz",
                     "fmri_sujet01_Lwhite_projection.time.gii.gz",
                     "sujet01_Lwhite.surf.gii.gz",
                     "sujet01_Lwhite.inflated.surf.gii.gz"
  )
  expected_files = file.path("BV_GIFTI", "GzipBase64", expected_files)
  out_files = file.path(outdir, expected_files)
  if (!all(file.exists(out_files)) || overwrite) {
    # url = paste0( "http://www.nitrc.org/frs/download.php",
    #               "/411/BV_GIFTI_1.3.tar.gz")
    url = "http://johnmuschelli.com/gifti/BV_GIFTI.tar.gz"

    destfile = basename(url)
    destfile = file.path(outdir, destfile)
    download.file( url = url, destfile = destfile)

    efiles = gsub("[.]gz$", "", expected_files)
    untar(tarfile = destfile,
          exdir = outdir,
          files = efiles)
    # compress them
    files_to_zip = file.path(outdir, efiles)
    sapply(files_to_zip, R.utils::gzip, compression = 9)
    file.remove(destfile)
  }
  return(out_files)
}

#' @title Check Presence of GIFTI Test Data
#' @description Checks if GIFTI test data is downloaded
#'
#' @param outdir Output directory for test file directory
#'
#' @return Logical indicator
#' @export
#' @examples
#' have_gifti_test_data(outdir = NULL)
have_gifti_test_data = function(
  outdir = system.file(package = "gifti")
) {
  # simple workaround for vignettes
  if (is.null(outdir)) {
    outdir = ""
  }
  if (outdir == "") {
    outdir = tempdir()
  }
  expected_files = c("sujet01_Lwhite.shape.gii.gz",
                     "fmri_sujet01_Lwhite_projection.time.gii.gz",
                     "sujet01_Lwhite.surf.gii.gz",
                     "sujet01_Lwhite.inflated.surf.gii.gz"
  )
  expected_files = file.path("BV_GIFTI", "GzipBase64", expected_files)
  out_files = file.path(outdir, expected_files)
  res = all(file.exists(out_files))
  return(res)
}

testthat::test_that("reading gifti files", {
  tdir = tempdir()
  download_gifti_data(tdir)
  expect_true(have_gifti_test_data(tdir))

  ######################################
  # Make sure you can read stuff in
  ######################################
  gii_files = download_gifti_data(tdir)
  gii_list = lapply(gii_files, readgii)

  ################################
  # Making sure the other read gifti
  # functions work
  ################################
  gii1 = readGIfTI(gii_files[1])
  gii2 = read_gifti(gii_files[1])

  expect_identical(gii1, gii2)
  expect_identical(gii_list[[1]], gii2)

  # Checking surf_triangles
  surf_files = grep("white[.]surf[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  surfs = lapply(surf_files, surf_triangles)

  expect_equal(names(surfs[[1]]), c("pointset", "triangle"))

  # Checking a shape file
  col_file = grep("white[.]shape[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  cdata = readgii(col_file)
  expect_equal(names(cdata$data), c("shape"))

  # Checking a shape file
  ts_file = grep("white_projection[.]time[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  tdata = readgii(ts_file)
  expect_equal(names(tdata$data), rep("timeseries", 128))

  ts_file = grep("white[.]inflated[.]surf[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  tdata = readgii(ts_file)
  expect_equal(names(tdata$data), c("pointset", "triangle"))
})

testthat::test_that("writing gifti files", {
  tdir = tempdir()
  download_gifti_data(tdir)
  expect_true(have_gifti_test_data(tdir))

  ######################################
  # Read files
  ######################################
  gii_files = download_gifti_data(tdir)
  gii_list = lapply(gii_files, readgii)

  ##########################################
  # Write files, read again, and check match
  ##########################################
  gii_out <- file.path(tdir, "written.gii")
  for (ii in 1:length(gii_list)) {
    gii1 <- gii_list[[ii]]

    # error #5
    gii1$data_info$Encoding <- ifelse(
      gii1$data_info$DataType == "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32",
      "ASCII",
      gii1$data_info$Encoding
    )

    writegii(gii1, gii_out)
    gii2 <- readgii(gii_out)

    # transformations is an XML pointer, which may differ
    gii1 <- gii1[names(gii1) != "transformations"]
    gii2 <- gii2[names(gii2) != "transformations"]

    expect_equal(gii1, gii2)
  }

  #################################################
  # Same as above, but write parsed transformations
  #################################################
  gii_out <- file.path(tdir, "written.gii")
  for (ii in 1:length(gii_list)) {
    gii1 <- gii_list[[ii]]

    # error #5
    gii1$data_info$Encoding <- ifelse(
      gii1$data_info$DataType == "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32",
      "ASCII",
      gii1$data_info$Encoding
    )

    writegii(gii1, gii_out, use_parsed_transformations = TRUE)
    gii2 <- readgii(gii_out)

    # transformations is an XML pointer, which may differ
    gii1 <- gii1[names(gii1) != "transformations"]
    gii2 <- gii2[names(gii2) != "transformations"]

    expect_equal(gii1, gii2)
  }
})

    14→GIFTI files have surface data that can be rendered in R.  Overall, they contain data that is stored in standard XML formats.  We provide some utilities that rely on the XML parsing of the `xml2` package, mainly the `readgii` function.  The `read_gifti` and `readGIfTI` functions are duplicates of the `readgii` function, but were made for ease of use for programmers who are used to the `readNIfTI` function from the `oro.nifti` package.

content.text:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the R gifti package, I can now provide a detailed report on the functions that need to be ported to Rust. Here's my analysis:

# Comprehensive Analysis of R gifti Package for Rust Port

## 1. Overview and File Structure

The R gifti package contains **16 R files** in `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/rust/gifti-rs/reference-gifti/R/` that implement functionality for reading and writing GIFTI (Geometry Format under the Neuroimaging Informatics Technology Initiative) files, which contain surface data for brain imaging.

## 2. Core Exported Functions (from NAMESPACE)

### Primary Functions:
- **`readgii`** / **`read_gifti`** / **`readGIfTI`** - Main reading functions
- **`writegii`** / **`write_gifti`** / **`writeGIfTI`** - Main writing functions
- **`is.gifti`** / **`is_gifti`** - Type checking functions

### Data Processing Functions:
- **`data_decoder`** - Decodes binary/encoded data from GIFTI files
- **`data_encoder`** - Encodes data for writing GIFTI files
- **`data_array_attributes`** - Extracts attributes from XML data arrays
- **`create_data_matrix`** - Creates matrices with proper ordering

### Utility Functions:
- **`convert_binary_datatype`** - Converts GIFTI data types to binary specifications
- **`convert_endian`** - Converts endianness specifications
- **`convert_intent`** - Converts GIFTI intent codes to readable names
- **`decompress_gii`** - Handles .gii.gz file decompression
- **`surf_triangles`** - Extracts triangular surface data for 3D rendering
- **`gifti_map_value`** - Maps values to triangular mesh coordinates
- **`gifti_list`** - Converts GIFTI to raw list format

### Test/Download Functions:
- **`download_gifti_data`** - Downloads test data
- **`have_gifti_test_data`** - Checks for test data availability

## 3. Function Complexity Analysis and Implementation Priority

### **Priority 1: Core Infrastructure (High Complexity)**

#### `readgii` (read_gifti.R)
- **Complexity**: Very High
- **Dependencies**: XML parsing, data decoding, endianness handling
- **Key Features**:
  - XML parsing with xml2 (needs XML parser in Rust)
  - Support for .gii.gz decompression
  - Metadata extraction from XML
  - Label table parsing
  - Multiple data array handling
  - Coordinate system transformations
  - Base64 and GZip decoding
  - Multiple encoding formats (ASCII, Base64Binary, GZipBase64Binary, ExternalFileBinary)

#### `writegii` (write_gifti.R)
- **Complexity**: Very High
- **Dependencies**: XML generation, data encoding
- **Key Features**:
  - XML document creation with proper GIFTI structure
  - DOCTYPE declaration handling
  - Metadata serialization
  - Label table serialization
  - Coordinate system transformation serialization
  - Data encoding in multiple formats

### **Priority 2: Data Processing (Medium-High Complexity)**

#### `data_decoder` (data_decoder.R)
- **Complexity**: High
- **Key Features**:
  - ASCII data parsing (space/newline separated)
  - Base64 decoding
  - GZip decompression
  - External file binary reading
  - Endianness handling
  - Multiple data type support (UINT8, INT32, UINT32, FLOAT32)

#### `data_encoder` (data_encoder.R)
- **Complexity**: High
- **Key Features**:
  - ASCII encoding with scientific notation control
  - Base64 encoding
  - GZip compression
  - Binary data serialization with endianness

#### `create_data_matrix` (create_data_matrix.R)
- **Complexity**: Medium
- **Key Features**:
  - Row-major vs column-major ordering
  - Multi-dimensional array handling (currently limited to 1D/2D)
  - Matrix reshaping

### **Priority 3: Utility Functions (Low-Medium Complexity)**

#### `convert_binary_datatype` (convert_binary_datatype.R)
- **Complexity**: Low
- **Key Features**:
  - Maps GIFTI data types to binary sizes and types
  - Supports: NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8, NIFTI_TYPE_INT32, NIFTI_TYPE_UINT32, NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32

#### `convert_intent` (convert_intent.R)
- **Complexity**: Low
- **Key Features**:
  - Large lookup table for NIFTI intent codes
  - Maps 40+ different intent types to readable names

#### `convert_endian` (convert_endian.R)
- **Complexity**: Very Low
- **Key Features**:
  - Simple string manipulation for endianness

#### `data_array_attributes` (data_array_attributes.R)
- **Complexity**: Medium
- **Key Features**:
  - XML attribute extraction
  - Dimension parsing and validation
  - Data frame construction

### **Priority 4: Surface Processing (Medium Complexity)**

#### `surf_triangles` (surf_triangles.R)
- **Complexity**: Medium
- **Key Features**:
  - Triangle mesh processing
  - Index adjustment (+1 for R's 1-based indexing)
  - Point set reordering based on triangles

#### `gifti_map_value` (gifti_map_value.R)
- **Complexity**: Medium
- **Key Features**:
  - Value mapping to triangular coordinates
  - Index validation and bounds checking

### **Priority 5: Utility and Test Functions (Low Complexity)**

#### `is.gifti` / `is_gifti` (is_gifti.R)
- **Complexity**: Very Low
- **Key Features**:
  - Simple type checking

#### `decompress_gii` (decompress_gii.R)
- **Complexity**: Low
- **Key Features**:
  - File extension checking
  - Gzip decompression

#### `gifti_list` (gifti_list.R)
- **Complexity**: Low
- **Key Features**:
  - XML to list conversion

## 4. R-Specific Features Requiring Special Attention

### **S3 Methods and Classes**
- **`gifti` class**: Custom S3 class for GIFTI objects
- **`is.gifti`**: S3 method for type checking
- **Class structure**: List with specific named elements

### **R-Specific Data Structures**
- **Named lists**: Extensive use of named lists for data organization
- **Data frames**: Used for metadata and attributes
- **Factors**: May appear in label tables
- **1-based indexing**: All indices are 1-based in R (needs adjustment for Rust's 0-based)

### **Matrix Operations**
- **`byrow` parameter**: Row-major vs column-major ordering
- **`aperm`**: Array permutation for dimension reordering
- **Matrix creation**: `matrix()` function with specific ordering

## 5. External Dependencies and FFI Requirements

### **Required Rust Crates**
- **XML Processing**: `quick-xml` or `roxmltree` for XML parsing/generation
- **Base64**: `base64` crate for encoding/decoding
- **Compression**: `flate2` for GZip compression/decompression
- **HTTP**: `reqwest` for downloading test data (if needed)
- **Serialization**: `serde` for structured data handling

### **No C/C++ FFI**
- The package uses only R's built-in functions and R packages
- No direct C/C++ foreign function interface calls
- All binary operations use R's `readBin`/`writeBin`

## 6. Performance-Critical Sections

### **Binary Data Processing**
- **Base64 encoding/decoding**: Large data arrays
- **GZip compression/decompression**: Memory-intensive operations
- **Binary serialization**: Endianness-aware operations
- **Matrix operations**: Large surface meshes with thousands of vertices

### **XML Processing**
- **Large XML documents**: GIFTI files can be substantial
- **Multiple data arrays**: Each file can contain multiple data arrays

## 3. Function Complexity Analysis and Implementation Priority

#### `readgii` (read_gifti.R)
- **Complexity**: Very High

  - XML parsing with xml2 (needs XML parser in Rust)
  - Support for .gii.gz decompression
  - Metadata extraction from XML
  - Label table parsing
  - Multiple data array handling
  - Coordinate system transformations
  - Base64 and GZip decoding
  - Multiple encoding formats (ASCII, Base64Binary, GZipBase64Binary, ExternalFileBinary)

#### `writegii` (write_gifti.R)
- **Complexity**: Very High
- **Dependencies**: XML generation, data encoding

#### `data_encoder` (data_encoder.R)
- **Complexity**: High
- **Key Features**:
  - ASCII encoding with scientific notation control
  - Base64 encoding
  - GZip compression
  - Binary data serialization with endianness

#### `create_data_matrix` (create_data_matrix.R)
- **Complexity**: Medium
- **Key Features**:
  - Row-major vs column-major ordering
  - Multi-dimensional array handling (currently limited to 1D/2D)
  - Matrix reshaping

### **Priority 3: Utility Functions (Low-Medium Complexity)**

#### `convert_binary_datatype` (convert_binary_datatype.R)

  - Maps 40+ different intent types to readable names

#### `convert_endian` (convert_endian.R)
- **Complexity**: Very Low
- **Key Features**:
  - Simple string manipulation for endianness

#### `data_array_attributes` (data_array_attributes.R)
- **Complexity**: Medium
- **Key Features**:
  - XML attribute extraction

  - Data frame construction

### **Priority 4: Surface Processing (Medium Complexity)**

- **Complexity**: Low

- **Complexity**: Low
- **Key Features**:

# GIFTI-RS Feature Parity Documentation

## Overview
This document tracks the feature parity between the R `gifti` package and the Rust `gifti-rs` implementation. The goal is to achieve 100% functional compatibility while leveraging Rust's performance and safety advantages.

## R Package Analysis

### Core Functionality (To be analyzed)
- [ ] GIFTI file reading/parsing
- [ ] GIFTI file writing/serialization
- [ ] XML structure validation
- [ ] Data array handling
- [ ] Metadata processing
- [ ] Error handling and validation

### R Functions to Port (Initial Assessment)
*Note: This section will be populated after examining the R package structure*

| R Function | Purpose | Complexity | Rust Equivalent | Status |
|------------|---------|------------|----------------|---------|
| TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | Pending |

### Data Structures to Port

| R Structure | Purpose | Rust Equivalent | Status |
|-------------|---------|----------------|---------|
| TBD | TBD | TBD | Pending |

## Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1: Core Data Structures
- [ ] Define GIFTI file structure types
- [ ] Implement XML parsing/serialization
- [ ] Create error handling framework
- [ ] Set up basic I/O operations

### Phase 2: Functional Compatibility
- [ ] Port reading functions
- [ ] Port writing functions
- [ ] Implement validation logic
- [ ] Add metadata handling

### Phase 3: Performance & Safety
- [ ] Optimize memory usage
- [ ] Add parallel processing where applicable
- [ ] Implement comprehensive error handling
- [ ] Add property-based testing

## Testing Strategy

### Test Coverage Goals
- [ ] Unit tests for all core functions
- [ ] Integration tests with R-generated fixtures
- [ ] Property-based tests for edge cases
- [ ] Performance benchmarks vs R implementation

### Cross-Language Validation
- [ ] Set up R test fixture generation
- [ ] Implement equivalence testing framework
- [ ] Add numerical tolerance handling
- [ ] Create performance comparison suite

## Current Status

### Completed Features
*None yet - project initialization phase*

### In Progress
- Project structure setup
- Documentation creation
- R package analysis planning

### Pending
- All core functionality
- Testing framework
- Performance optimization
- Documentation completion

## Success Metrics

### Functional Parity
- [ ] 100% R function coverage
- [ ] Identical output for all test cases
- [ ] Complete error handling compatibility
- [ ] Full metadata preservation

### Performance Goals
- [ ] ≤20% performance difference from R (or faster)
- [ ] Memory usage optimization
- [ ] Parallel processing where beneficial
- [ ] Zero-cost abstractions

### Quality Assurance
- [ ] >95% test coverage
- [ ] Zero unsafe code (or fully documented)
- [ ] Comprehensive rustdoc documentation
- [ ] Passes all clippy lints

## Dependencies

### Core Dependencies (Planned)
- `serde` + `serde_xml_rs` - XML serialization
- `thiserror` - Error handling
- `ndarray` - Multi-dimensional arrays
- `byteorder` - Binary data handling

### Development Dependencies (Planned)
- `criterion` - Benchmarking
- `proptest` - Property-based testing
- `tempfile` - Test utilities
- `approx` - Numerical comparisons

## Notes
- This document will be updated as the R package analysis progresses
- Feature complexity will be assessed during implementation
- Performance benchmarks will be added after core functionality is complete

Based on the search results, I can provide information about the GIFTI file format and its XML schema/DTD specification in neuroimaging:

## GIFTI File Format Overview

GIFTI is a geometry format under the Neuroimaging Informatics Technology Initiative (NIfTI). The file organization is based on the XML format, which stores data arrays.

## DTD Specification

The search results indicate that there is a gifti.dtd file available as part of the GIFTI specification. The GIFTI File Format specification was released on January 14, 2011, and is available as GIFTI_Surface_Format.pdf.

## XML Schema Structure

The Gifti specification was available as a PDF download from http://www.nitrc.org/projects/gifti/, with quotes from the gifti spec dated 2011-01-14. The format includes several key components:

- **Data Arrays**: The numeric data and its related metadata are contained in DataArray elements, with CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix child elements used when the DataArray's Intent is NIFTI_INTENT_POINTSET

- **Coordinate Systems**: For DataArrays with NIFTI_INTENT_POINTSET intent, elements describe the stereotaxic space before and after transformation matrix application, with Talairach Space being the most common, and at least one CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix required

## File Types and Usage

GIFTI files are used for different types of neuroimaging data:

- Geometry data (surf.gii) represents a surface mesh storing 3D coordinates and triangle arrays of a surface
- Metric files (func.gii and shape.gii) store continuous values associated with vertices as one-dimensional vectors of intensities  
- Label data (label.gii) stores integer values for each vertex together with a name and a colour

## Technical Implementation

The default encoding is GIFTI_ENCODING_B64GZ, and data types are limited to NIFTI types including NIFTI_TYPE_UINT8 (unsigned 8-bit bytes), NIFTI_TYPE_INT32 (signed 32-bit integers), and NIFTI_TYPE_FLOAT32 (32-bit single precision floating point).

The GIFTI format provides a standardized XML-based approach for storing and exchanging neuroimaging geometry and surface data, with a formal DTD specification that defines the structure and validation rules for these files.

input.prompt: Please extract the complete DTD specification for GIFTI XML files, including all element definitions, attributes, and structure requirements.

content.content:
Here's the complete DTD specification for GIFTI XML files:

Root Element: GIFTI
Structure:
- Optional MetaData
- Optional LabelTable
- One or more DataArray (required)

Key Elements:
- LabelTable: Contains zero or more Label elements
- DataArray: Contains optional MetaData, optional CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix, and required Data
- MetaData: Contains zero or more MD (Name/Value) elements
- CoordinateSystemTransformMatrix: Includes DataSpace, TransformedSpace, and MatrixData

GIFTI Attributes:
- xmlns:xsi (optional)
- xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation (optional)
- NumberOfDataArrays (required)
- Version (required)

DataArray Attributes Include:
- ArrayIndexingOrder (RowMajor/ColumnMajor)
- DataType (UINT8, INT32, FLOAT32)
- Dimensionality
- Dimension specifications (Dim0-Dim5)
- Encoding (ASCII, Base64Binary, GZipBase64Binary, ExternalFileBinary)
- Endian (BigEndian/LittleEndian)
- Intent (multiple statistical and data representation types)

Label Attributes:
- Index (required)
- Red, Green, Blue, Alpha (optional color components)

file.content:
testthat::test_that("reading gifti files", {
  tdir = tempdir()
  download_gifti_data(tdir)
  expect_true(have_gifti_test_data(tdir))

  ######################################
  # Make sure you can read stuff in
  ######################################
  gii_files = download_gifti_data(tdir)
  gii_list = lapply(gii_files, readgii)

  ################################
  # Making sure the other read gifti
  # functions work
  ################################
  gii1 = readGIfTI(gii_files[1])
  gii2 = read_gifti(gii_files[1])

  expect_identical(gii1, gii2)
  expect_identical(gii_list[[1]], gii2)

  # Checking surf_triangles
  surf_files = grep("white[.]surf[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  surfs = lapply(surf_files, surf_triangles)

  expect_equal(names(surfs[[1]]), c("pointset", "triangle"))

  # Checking a shape file
  col_file = grep("white[.]shape[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  cdata = readgii(col_file)
  expect_equal(names(cdata$data), c("shape"))

  # Checking a shape file
  ts_file = grep("white_projection[.]time[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  tdata = readgii(ts_file)
  expect_equal(names(tdata$data), rep("timeseries", 128))

  ts_file = grep("white[.]inflated[.]surf[.]gii", gii_files, value = TRUE)
  tdata = readgii(ts_file)
  expect_equal(names(tdata$data), c("pointset", "triangle"))
})

file.content:
testthat::test_that("writing gifti files", {
  tdir = tempdir()
  download_gifti_data(tdir)
  expect_true(have_gifti_test_data(tdir))

  ######################################
  # Read files
  ######################################
  gii_files = download_gifti_data(tdir)
  gii_list = lapply(gii_files, readgii)

  ##########################################
  # Write files, read again, and check match
  ##########################################
  gii_out <- file.path(tdir, "written.gii")
  for (ii in 1:length(gii_list)) {
    gii1 <- gii_list[[ii]]

    # error #5
    gii1$data_info$Encoding <- ifelse(
      gii1$data_info$DataType == "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32",
      "ASCII",
      gii1$data_info$Encoding
    )

    writegii(gii1, gii_out)
    gii2 <- readgii(gii_out)

    # transformations is an XML pointer, which may differ
    gii1 <- gii1[names(gii1) != "transformations"]
    gii2 <- gii2[names(gii2) != "transformations"]

    expect_equal(gii1, gii2)
  }

  #################################################
  # Same as above, but write parsed transformations
  #################################################
  gii_out <- file.path(tdir, "written.gii")
  for (ii in 1:length(gii_list)) {
    gii1 <- gii_list[[ii]]

    # error #5
    gii1$data_info$Encoding <- ifelse(
      gii1$data_info$DataType == "NIFTI_TYPE_INT32",
      "ASCII",
      gii1$data_info$Encoding
    )

    writegii(gii1, gii_out, use_parsed_transformations = TRUE)
    gii2 <- readgii(gii_out)

    # transformations is an XML pointer, which may differ
    gii1 <- gii1[names(gii1) != "transformations"]
    gii2 <- gii2[names(gii2) != "transformations"]

    expect_equal(gii1, gii2)
  }
})

[package]
name = "gifti"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
authors = ["Claude <claude@anthropic.com>"]
description = "Fast, safe Rust implementation of GIFTI (Geometry Format under the Neuroimaging Informatics Technology Initiative) file format"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/user/gifti-rs"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/gifti"
homepage = "https://github.com/user/gifti-rs"
readme = "README.md"
categories = ["science", "data-structures", "parsing"]
keywords = ["neuroimaging", "gifti", "xml", "nifti", "brain"]

[dependencies]
# XML processing
quick-xml = { version = "0.31", features = ["serialize"] }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }

# Data structures
ndarray = "0.15"
indexmap = "2.0"

# Binary data handling
base64 = "0.21"
byteorder = "1.5"

# Compression
flate2 = "1.0"

# Error handling
thiserror = "1.0"

# Logging
log = "0.4"

# File I/O
memmap2 = "0.9"

[dev-dependencies]
approx = "0.5"
tempfile = "3.8"
env_logger = "0.10"
criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }
proptest = "1.4"

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []
parallel = ["ndarray/rayon"]

[[bench]]
name = "performance"
harness = false

[profile.release]
lto = true
codegen-units = 1

## Summary

I've successfully completed the initial phases of the GIFTI-RS project setup and design:

### ✅ Completed Tasks:
1. **Project Documentation** - Created comprehensive `feature_parity.md` and `progress.md` files
2. **R Package Analysis** - Thoroughly examined the reference R package structure and functionality
3. **GIFTI Format Analysis** - Studied the XML DTD specification and format requirements
4. **Rust Architecture Design** - Created the core library structure with proper type system

### 🏗️ Architecture Implemented:
- **Core Type System** - Complete enum definitions for DataType, Encoding, Endianness, Intent
- **Error Handling** - Comprehensive error types with proper error propagation
- **Metadata System** - Structures for handling file metadata and label tables
- **Project Structure** - Organized module layout following Rust best practices

### 📊 Key Findings:
- **23 R functions** need to be ported with varying complexity levels
- **Priority-based implementation** strategy with 5 phases
- **XML-based format** with multiple encoding options (ASCII, Base64, GZip)
- **Strong type safety** opportunities in Rust vs R's dynamic typing

### 🎯 Next Steps:
Ready to proceed with implementing the core GIFTI reading functionality, which will include:
- XML parsing and validation
- Data decoding for multiple formats
- Binary data handling with endianness
- Integration with the type system

The project is well-structured and follows the R2Rust unified conversion guide, prioritizing feature parity and performance while leveraging Rust's safety guarantees.
